title: Mock Exam C
description: A 22-task RHCSA9 mock exam covering persistent networking, repositories, users, services, storage, NFS, SSH, and rootless containers across client and server.

tasks: On client, recover root access from the console and set the root password to cinder9.

On client, configure persistent IPv4 networking.

IP address: 192.168.122.42/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: client-c.exam9.lab
On client, configure enabled BaseOS and AppStream repositories from http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
On client, install lsof from the configured repositories and remove dos2unix if it is installed.
On client, create group opsc9. Create users anac9, devc9, and auditc9; auditc9 must use /sbin/nologin. Set each password to cinder9 and add anac9 and devc9 to opsc9.
On client, set maximum password age 60 days and warning period 7 days for anac9. Allow members of opsc9 to run /usr/bin/systemctl with sudo without a password.
On client, create /srv/opsc9 owned by root:opsc9 with permissions 2770 and a default ACL granting opsc9 full access.
On client, create executable script /usr/local/bin/report-c9 that writes the active state of sshd, chronyd, and firewalld to /root/report-c9.txt.
On client, create a 512 MiB swap file at /swapc9, activate it immediately, and make it persistent.
On client, create volume group vgc9 on /dev/sdb, create logical volume datac9 with size 320 MiB, format it as XFS, and mount it persistently at /mnt/datac9.
On client, create user podc9, enable lingering for that user, and run a rootless container named webc9 from localhost/rhcsa-httpd-base:latest.

On server, configure persistent IPv4 networking.

IP address: 192.168.122.3/24
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: server-c.exam9.lab
On server, configure enabled BaseOS and AppStream repositories from http://server/repo/BaseOS/ and http://server/repo/AppStream/ with GPG checks disabled.
On server, create group srvc9, create user svcc9 with password cinder9, add svcc9 to srvc9, and allow srvc9 to run /usr/bin/systemctl with sudo without a password.
On server, publish /var/www/html/exam-c.html containing RHCSA9-C, configure httpd to listen on TCP port 8302, label the port for httpd, and open it permanently in firewalld.
On server, enable persistent systemd journal storage and restart systemd-journald.
On server, create and enable auditc9.timer so it runs every 7 minutes and appends server-c to /var/log/auditc9.log.
On server, set the default boot target to multi-user.target and create /srv/server-c9 owned by root:srvc9 with permissions 2770.
On server, export /exports/rhcsa9-c to 192.168.122.0/24. On client, mount server:/exports/rhcsa9-c persistently at /mnt/rhcsa9-c.
On server, create user copyc9 with password cinder9. On client, configure key-based SSH login for root to copyc9@server.
On client, create /root/exam-c-copy.txt containing RHCSA9-C and copy it to server:/home/copyc9/exam-c-copy.txt.
On server, enable chronyd for the lab network. On client, configure chronyd to use server as its only time source.
solution_commands: # At the boot menu, edit the selected kernel entry.
# Append rw init=/bin/bash to the linux line and boot with Ctrl+x.
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.42/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
for service in sshd chronyd firewalld; do
nmcli connection modify "System eth1" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.3/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes
checks: CONN="System eth1"; nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.42/24 && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.1 && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fq 192.168.122.3

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; hostnamectl --static | grep -qx server-c.exam9.lab && CONN="System eth1"; nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.3/24 && nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fqx 192.168.122.1 && nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN" | grep -Fq 192.168.122.3'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; test -f /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '"'"'^baseurl=http://server/repo/BaseOS/?$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && grep -Eq '"'"'^baseurl=http://server/repo/AppStream/?$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo && [ "$(grep -Ec '"'"'^enabled=1$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ] && [ "$(grep -Ec '"'"'^gpgcheck=0$'"'"' /etc/yum.repos.d/rhcsa9-exam.repo)" -ge 2 ]'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; getent group srvc9 >/dev/null && id -nG svcc9 | tr '"'"' '"'"' '"'"'\n'"'"' | grep -qx srvc9 && grep -Eq '"'"'^%srvc9[[:space:]]+ALL=\(ALL\)[[:space:]]+NOPASSWD:[[:space:]]*/usr/bin/systemctl$'"'"' /etc/sudoers.d/srvc9-systemctl'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq RHCSA9-C /var/www/html/exam-c.html && grep -Eq '"'"'^Listen[[:space:]]+8302$'"'"' /etc/httpd/conf.d/exam-c.conf && semanage port -l | awk '"'"'$1 == "http_port_t" && $2 == "tcp" && $0 ~ /(^|[ ,])8302([, ]|$)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' && firewall-cmd --permanent --query-port=8302/tcp && systemctl is-enabled httpd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active httpd | grep -qx active'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; files=""; test -d /var/log/journal && systemctl is-active systemd-journald | grep -qx active && files=$(find /etc/systemd -maxdepth 2 \( -path /etc/systemd/journald.conf -o -path '"'"'/etc/systemd/journald.conf.d/*.conf'"'"' \) -type f 2>/dev/null); test -n "$files" && awk '"'"'FNR == 1 {in_journal=0} /^[[:space:]]*\[Journal\][[:space:]]*($|#)/ {in_journal=1; next} /^[[:space:]]*\[/ {in_journal=0} in_journal && /^[[:space:]]*Storage[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*persistent[[:space:]]*($|#)/ {found=1} END {exit !found}'"'"' $files'

ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled auditc9.timer | grep -qx enabled && grep -Eq '"'"'^OnCalendar=\*:0/7$'"'"' /etc/systemd/system/auditc9.timer && grep -Fxq '"'"'echo server-c >> /var/log/auditc9.log'"'"' /usr/local/sbin/auditc9.sh'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl get-default | grep -qx multi-user.target && stat -c '"'"'%U:%G:%a'"'"' /srv/server-c9 | grep -qx root:srvc9:2770'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Eq "^/exports/rhcsa9-c[[:space:]]+192\\.168\\.122\\.0/24" /etc/exports.d/rhcsa9-c.exports && systemctl is-active nfs-server | grep -qx active'
runuser -l root -c 'ssh -o BatchMode=yes -o NumberOfPasswordPrompts=0 -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null copyc9@server true'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; grep -Fxq RHCSA9-C /home/copyc9/exam-c-copy.txt'
ssh server bash -lc 'set -euo pipefail; systemctl is-enabled chronyd | grep -qx enabled && systemctl is-active chronyd | grep -qx active'
